title: tagetes maculata
intro: Tagetes maculata, also known as the Mexican marigold or signet marigold, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its bright orange and yellow daisy-like flowers with dark spots. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tagetes maculata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
